title: Kadhal Rojave Lyrics Roja
stanzas:
thendral ennai theendinaal
selai theendum niyabagam
chinn pookkal paarkayil
dhegam paartha niyabagam

velli odai pesinaal
sonna vaarthai niyabagam
megam rendu sergayil
mogam konda niyabagam

vaayillaamal ponaal
vaarthai illai pennae
nee illaamal ponaal
vazhkai illai kannae

veesugindra thendrale
velai illai nindru po
pesugindra vennilaa
penmai illai oindhu po

poo valartha thottamae
koondhal illai theerndhu po
boomi paarkkum vaaname
pulliyaaga theindhu po

paavai illai paavai
thaevai enna thaevai
jeevan pona pinna
saevai enna saevai

seo.title: Kadhal Rojave Lyrics Roja

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kadhal Rojave?

The song explores themes of love and longing, capturing the emotional turmoil of separation.

In which movie does Kadhal Rojave appear?

The song is featured in the movie Roja, released in 1992.

Who composed the music for Kadhal Rojave?

The music was composed by the legendary AR Rahman.

Who are the singers of Kadhal Rojave?

The song is beautifully sung by SPB and Sujatha Mohan.

What is the significance of Roja in Tamil cinema?

Roja is a landmark film known for its music and its portrayal of love amidst political turmoil.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the film Roja, Kadhal Rojave is a poignant melody that reflects the deep yearning and emotional pain of separation between the lead characters. The song beautifully conveys the essence of love and longing through its soulful lyrics by Vairamuthu and the hauntingly beautiful music by AR Rahman. Sung by SPB and Sujatha Mohan, it evokes a sense of nostalgia and melancholy, making it a timeless classic. The emotions captured in the song resonate with anyone who has experienced the ache of being away from a loved one.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the timeless magic of Kadhal Rojave from the iconic film Roja. This enchanting duet by SPB and Sujatha Mohan, with Vairamuthu's poetic lyrics and AR Rahman's mesmerizing music, captures the essence of longing and love. Dive into the soulful melody that continues to resonate with hearts across generations.
stanzaMeanings: Love, my rose, where are you? Tears are flowing, tears.
You are in my eyes, you are in my tears. Even when I close my eyes, you are in my heart.
What is this? Tell me, tell me.
When the breeze touches me, I remember the touch of your saree. When I see small flowers, I remember your body.
When the silver stream talks, I remember the words you said. When two clouds meet, I remember the desire.
Without a mouth, there are no words. Without you, there is no life.
Are kisses with thorns? Tell me, tell me.
Love, my rose, where are you? Tears are flowing, tears.
You are in my eyes, you are in my tears. Even when I close my eyes, you are in my heart.
What is this? Tell me, tell me.
The blowing breeze has no work, it stops. The talking moonlight has no femininity, it fades.
The garden that grows flowers has no hair, it ends. The sky that looks at the earth becomes dotted.
Without a doll, what is the need for a doll? After life is gone, what is the need for service?
Are kisses with thorns? Tell me, tell me.
Love, my rose, where are you? Tears are flowing, tears.
You are in my eyes, you are in my tears. Even when I close my eyes, you are in my heart.
What is this? Tell me, tell me.
